title: Tennis for Two debuts
description: Physicist William Higinbotham creates Tennis for Two on an oscilloscope at Brookhaven National Laboratory — one of the earliest video games ever made.

title: Spacewar! is created at MIT
description: Steve Russell and colleagues build Spacewar! on a PDP-1, the first widely distributed and influential digital computer game.

title: Magnavox Odyssey launches
description: The first commercial home video game console arrives, bringing interactive play to television sets for the first time.
impact: Established the home console as a product category.

title: Atari releases Pong
description: Pong becomes the first commercially successful arcade video game and launches Atari as an industry force.

title: Atari 2600 released
description: The cartridge-based Atari 2600 popularizes interchangeable games and dominates the home market.

title: Space Invaders ignites arcade craze
description: Taito's Space Invaders sparks a global arcade phenomenon and reportedly causes a coin shortage in Japan.

title: Pac-Man releases
description: Namco's Pac-Man becomes a cultural icon and one of the highest-grossing arcade games of all time.

title: Donkey Kong introduces Mario
description: Shigeru Miyamoto's Donkey Kong debuts 'Jumpman' — later renamed Mario — launching gaming's most famous mascot.

title: The North American video game crash
description: Market saturation and low-quality titles trigger a collapse that wipes out much of the U.S. console industry.
impact: Reset the industry and opened the door for Nintendo's revival.

title: NES launches in North America
description: Nintendo revives the home console market with the NES and Super Mario Bros.
impact: Rebuilt the console market after the 1983 crash.

title: Game Boy launches with Tetris
description: Nintendo's Game Boy popularizes handheld gaming, bundled with the addictive puzzle game Tetris.

title: Sonic the Hedgehog debuts
description: Sega launches its speedy mascot to challenge Nintendo, intensifying the 16-bit console war.

title: DOOM redefines the FPS
description: id Software's DOOM popularizes the first-person shooter, networked multiplayer, and the shareware distribution model.
impact: Established the FPS genre and PC gaming as a cultural force.

title: Sony PlayStation launches in Japan
description: Sony enters the console market with a CD-based system that reshapes the industry.
impact: Brought 3D, CD media, and mature games to the mainstream.

title: Super Mario 64 defines 3D platforming
description: The Nintendo 64 launch title sets the template for analog 3D movement and camera control.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
description: Widely regarded as one of the greatest games ever made, pioneering Z-targeting and 3D action-adventure design.

title: PlayStation 2 released
description: The PS2 launches and goes on to become the best-selling home console of all time.

title: Microsoft launches the Xbox
description: Microsoft enters the console business with Halo: Combat Evolved and a built-in hard drive.
impact: Set the stage for modern online console gaming.

title: Xbox Live launches
description: Microsoft's unified online service brings matchmaking, friends lists, and downloads to consoles.

title: Valve releases Steam
description: Steam debuts as a digital distribution platform, eventually transforming how PC games are sold and played.

title: World of Warcraft launches
description: Blizzard's MMORPG becomes a cultural juggernaut with millions of subscribers.

title: Nintendo Wii brings motion control
description: The Wii's motion controls broaden gaming's audience and become a worldwide sensation.

title: The iPhone launches mobile gaming
description: The App Store era begins shortly after, opening an enormous new market for mobile games.

title: League of Legends releases
description: Riot Games' MOBA becomes a cornerstone of competitive esports for the next decade.

title: Twitch launches
description: Live game streaming explodes in popularity, creating new careers and viewing habits.
impact: Made game streaming a mainstream entertainment medium.

title: Minecraft 1.0 releases
description: Mojang's sandbox game launches officially and becomes the best-selling video game of all time.

title: PS4 and Xbox One launch
description: The eighth generation begins with a renewed focus on online services and indie games.

title: Nintendo Switch launches
description: Nintendo's hybrid console debuts with Breath of the Wild and becomes a runaway success.
impact: Reinvented Nintendo with a hybrid home/handheld design.

title: Fortnite Battle Royale releases
description: Epic Games' free-to-play battle royale becomes a global cultural phenomenon.

title: Fortnite World Cup awards record prizes
description: The Fortnite World Cup hands out a $30M prize pool, with solo winner Bugha taking $3M.

title: PS5 and Xbox Series X|S launch
description: The ninth generation arrives with ultra-fast SSDs and ray tracing during a global pandemic.
impact: Brought SSD-class streaming and ray tracing to consoles.

title: Tears of the Kingdom breaks records
description: The Zelda sequel sells over 10 million copies in three days, a record for the franchise.
